title: agropyron biflorum subsp. andinum
intro: Agropyron biflorum subsp. andinum, also known as the Andean wheat grass, is a subspecies of the Agropyron biflorum plant, which is a type of grass native to the Andean region. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, inflammation, and respiratory problems. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce symptoms of various health conditions, including fever, cough, and sore throat. Agropyron biflorum subsp. andinum can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be taken internally to promote overall health and well-being.
